title: Crispy Korean Cabbage Salad
description: A garlicky, sweet‑savory Korean cabbage salad with a caramelized treacle glaze, crisp textures, and a touch of heat.
instructions: Wash all vegetables thoroughly and pat dry.
Shred the Napa cabbage into 1‑inch strips and place in a large bowl.
Add the julienned carrot, sliced red bell pepper, and minced garlic to the cabbage.
In a small bowl whisk together soy sauce, rice vinegar, treacle, sesame oil, lime juice, gochugaru, and a pinch of salt to make the glaze.
Pour the glaze over the vegetables and toss to coat evenly.
Heat a large non‑stick skillet over medium‑high heat and add a splash of sesame oil.
Add the cabbage mixture to the skillet, spreading it out in a single layer.
Cook, stirring occasionally, for 3–4 minutes until the edges begin to crisp but the cabbage remains mostly raw.
If desired, give the salad a final toss to distribute any browned bits.
Transfer the salad to a serving bowl, sprinkle with toasted sesame seeds, and serve immediately.
